review_text:
Good service. Great food. The owner was walking around the dining room the whole time. Table side guacamole was incredible. Oysters appetizer was a bit much. We had 6 plain and 6 preparados. Maybe the oyster are out of season. My wife had the deep-fried snapper. It was quite tasty with a crunchy skin. I had the Charola Levantamuertos. Omg! I was in heaven. What a variety of tastes! Crabs were done perfectly. Shrimp, langostinos, mussels and octopus in a red sauce. It was splendid. Any hotter would have been painful. Perfect.

Service was as prompt as we wanted. Being out with my wife, we really didn't want to be doted on. She was prompt and cared for our comfort. Good.

The only reason I don't give 5 stars is that they don't have iced tea. Really? Mexican food without iced tea? Specifically iced tea with lime.

We both look forward to eating here again.

review_text: Someone recommended this place to me because my favorite Mexican restaurant took one of my favorite dishes off the menu. Once I walked through the door I was excited at the menu but that ended quickly. We waited about 8 minutes to be seated in a completely empty restaurant. Once we sat down we had a waitress that didn't speak English which was no issue but they changed to one that did and she took forever to come. My wife is a vegetarian so she asked if she could just get cheese on the Quesadilla or add veggies. They said no only what's on the menu even though it says you can choose your own meat (so how is no meat not an option). Once again service was very slow in an empty restaurant (another family came towards the end of our meal). I had hope that the food would heal all buy sadly no. The food wasn't good, the mango drink I ordered was clearly watered down, the fish in my soup tasted very strange. The only thing that was good was the Churro my son ordered to go. Our meal came out to around $70, that felt like a $20-30 meal (mind you my wife didn't order anything). From what I read from other reviews there is a 15% tip added to your bill and we still tipped. Even my son (who likes everything) was disappointed. Sometimes I feel like I might have just went on a bad day but not this time. We will never go back to this place to eat. I'll probably just order my son a Churro to go because that was the one saving grace. I'm not sure why but it felt like we were being treated very bad from the moment we walked in.

review_text:
There are plenty of places to get good Mexican food in this part of Detroit, but if you want seafood, this is the place.

There is a large parking area behind the restaurant, which has a bright blue, colorful sign. Great logo, it would look good on a hat.

Right away you know something is different when instead of getting a dish of salsa with a chip basket you get a dish of diced tomatoes, onions, cilantro with surimi. They let you know right away, this is a sea food place (although they do have other options).

Very well stocked bar, and lots of drink options. Their selections of Micheladas is astounding, featuring some huge options packed with shrimp, crab, etc.

I recommend either looking at the menu ahead of time, or leaving yourself some extra time. It is large, and has many tempting options.

We tried something familiar, Camarones Mexicana (shrimp with tomatoes, onions and just a few jalapeños) and something new to us, a bed of rice with a spicy stew of shrimp, octopus and surimi (we were warned it was spicy, but the balance of heat and flavor was perfect). Everything was excellent.

This is a great place to try an aspect of Mexican cuisine that is often under represented in many restaurants, stop in and try something, you might get a new favorite.
owner_response: Thank you, Jeffrey, for your wonderful review. We're glad to hear that our unique seafood offerings stood out for you. Also, happy to learn that you appreciated our extensive drink options and our careful approach to spice balance. We're all about delivering excellent experiences and we're incredibly proud of our menu variety. Thank you again for your insightful feedback!

review_text:
I recently visited Mi Lindo San Blas Detroit and I have mixed feelings about my experience.

Food: 10/10
The food was amazing fresh, hot, and full of flavor. Definitely one of the best parts of the visit.

Atmosphere:
This time around, the atmosphere felt a little off. In the past, it’s always been lively with music or even a singer, and everyone seemed happy to be there. This visit felt more quiet and a bit boring compared to what I’m used to.

Service:
Unfortunately, the service really missed the mark. Our waitress didn’t make us feel welcome at all. She only came to our table twice. I asked for more chips and never received them. I even stopped another waitress to ask for chips and ceviche  she said she would bring them, but never did. Overall, the staff seemed disengaged and not very attentive.

Overall:
The food alone would keep me coming back, but the service and atmosphere need improvement. A little more friendliness and attentiveness would make a huge difference in the overall experience.
owner_response: Thank you for your honest feedback, Sirena. We're delighted you enjoyed our food but are disappointed to hear about your service and atmosphere experience. We have shared your comments with our team in the interest of improvement. We would like the chance to discuss this further. Please contact us at promotions@L3amigos.com.
